profile.name: Dr. P. Mathan Kumar
profile.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
profile.bio: Passionate about research on thin film fabrication and nanomaterial synthesis for electrochemical applications.

mentors.name: Dr. B. Muthuraaman
mentors.role: Head of the Department, Department of Energy

mentors.name: Dr. Ranjith G Nair
mentors.role: Assistant Professor, Department of Applied Physics

experience.title: University Research Fellow
experience.description: Sulfurization of metal of Metal Oxides using Hydrothermal method; Preparation of Counter electrodes; Fabrication of lab Scale Dye Sensitized Solar Cell device; Achieved a higher photovoltaic performance with 7.09 % efficiency.

experience.title: Project fellow - MHRD RUSA
experience.description: Preparation of Bimetallic Tungstate using Hydrothermal method; Tested its electrocatalytic activity using electrochemical techniques; Designed Microwave assisted reflux & Spray pyrolysis setup; Collaborated with researchers in photocatalysts materials; Familiar with XRD, XPS, TEM, FESEM and BET characterization techniques.

experience.title: Research Fellow
experience.description: Preparation of Thin films via RF sputtering technique at HHV Pvt. Ltd.; Explored the Ion implantation techniques using Gas and Metal sources; Performed Gamma Irradiation experiment for prepared thin films.

academic.description: Thesis: Investigation on the Structural, Morphological, Surface, and Electrochemical Properties of Hydrothermally Sulfurized Metal Oxides for it’s Application in Dye Sensitized Solar Cells

academic.institution: Ayya Nadar Janaki Ammal College, Sivakasi, Tamil Nadu, India
Kalasalingam College of Arts & Science, Krishnankoil, Tamil Nadu, India

research.title: Dye Sensitized Solar Cells
research.description: Research focusing on thin film fabrication and nanomaterial synthesis for Dye Sensitized Solar Cells (DSSC).

research.title: Photocatalyst
research.description: Development and analysis of photocatalytic materials for environmental and energy applications.

research.title: Photoelectrochemistry
research.description: Investigation into structural, morphological, and electrochemical properties of sulfurized metal oxides.
